---
title: "Table"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# --- 2.1 Sequencing Summary Table (Table 1) ---

# Make sure the 'gt' package is loaded
library(gt)

# 1. Define the read counts for each sample after quality control
illumina_reads <- c(79689, 95146, 88794, 69149) # counts of non-chimeric reads
ont_reads <- c(102299, 107381, 121916, 87402) # read counts from EPI2ME wf-16S report

## illumina_reads <- c(47974, 75906, 67665, 61549)
## ont_reads <- c(10370, 109020, 122926, 90338)

# 2. Create a summary data frame
read_summary_data <- tibble(
  Platform = c("Illumina", "ONT"),
  Mean_Reads = c(mean(illumina_reads), mean(ont_reads)),
  SD_Reads = c(sd(illumina_reads), sd(ont_reads))
) %>%
  # Create the final display string "mean ± sd"
  dplyr::mutate(
    `Mean Reads per Sample (± SD)` = paste0(
      # format() with big.mark adds a comma for thousands, making it easier to read
      format(round(Mean_Reads, 0), big.mark = ","),
      " ± ",
      format(round(SD_Reads, 0), big.mark = ",")
    )
  ) %>%
  # Keep only the columns we need for the final table
  dplyr::select(Platform, `Mean Reads per Sample (± SD)`)


# 3. Create the styled gt table
read_summary_table_gt <- read_summary_data %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Table 1 Summary of Sequencing Reads per Sample"
  ) %>%
  # Center-align all columns
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = everything()
  ) %>%
  # Apply modern table options
  tab_options(
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    table_body.hlines.style = "none",
    column_labels.border.bottom.style = "solid",
    column_labels.border.bottom.width = px(2),
    table.border.top.style = "none",
    table.border.bottom.style = "none"
  )

# 4. Save and display the table
gtsave(read_summary_table_gt, "tables/Table1_read_summary.png")
gtsave(read_summary_table_gt, "tables/Table1_read_summary.rtf")
read_summary_table_gt
```
